evidence_text: Fourth time's the charm, and that's the one that matters most, doesn't it? Yeah, I mean, they're an unbelievable team. They're very good, and mentally for us to kind of get over that hurdle against Campbell and show up the way we did today, getting down early in the first quarter and the resolve that we had in the second quarter, we were great defensively second quarter and gave ourselves a chance in the second half, so just very proud of the girls. What was going wrong there early when you were down 14-3? We just were flustered a little bit, and we didn't settle in yet, and kind of had butterflies, I think, and we weren't making reads on our offense. We were kind of guessing on stuff, but the girls settled in and bought into what our game plan was, and we had a lot of kids step up. Defensively, again, we were great in the second quarter, but they were doubling Mesa, and we kind of recognized that, and we had kids make shots when they were open. You know, Avery shot the lights out today. Cam hit big ones. It just was a team effort. We had to have everybody we had them. You had that hot finish there the second quarter. How did you try to make sure that you didn't have the big letdown coming out of halftime? Well, we just talked at half about how we got through that quarter because we were really good defensively. We got after them a little bit, and our defense turned into offense. And we tried to carry that through the second half. And Campbell came in, and they made a lot of shots. And Cammie Curtis finishes with dang near 30, I think. But we limited everybody else. We were great on the basketball. It was our defense that carried us through the game. Obviously, we shot the ball well, but we had to get stops, and we got them. As much as nobody likes to lose, was maybe losing that regional championship game kind of a blessing in disguise considering how the state bracket was shaped and how you went through it? We had to beat really good teams to win, and we knew that
